title: R-Ladies Montreal: Wishes you a Merry Christmas
description:
Hello Everyone,

The Holiday season is here and due to these happy family days we have decided to skip this event and start over on January 17th of 2019.

We are happy to wish you a Merry Christmas and a wonderful and prosperous New Year 2019.

May all your coding and wishes come true.

See you next year,

RLadies Montreal.

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Data Cleaning &Introduction to Social Network Analysis.
description:
This upcoming Thursday we will be learning and discussing about Data cleaning and Social Network Analysis. We are excited to be receiving both presentations by 2 lovely members of the RLadies Montreal, Jessica Renaud and Jennifer Yu.

Remember to update R & Rstudio to the newest version and to install (if not already) :
install.packages("readxl")
install.packages("dplyr")
install.packages("tidyr")
install.packages("igraph")

Food and Drinks will be provided at location.

We can't wait to see you on Thursday!

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Introduction to Shiny
description: The session will include an introduction to shiny https://shiny.rstudio.com/ Please come with R and the shiny package installed!

title: September Meetup - Welcome back!
description: We'll hold a planning session where we set the objectives for the fall session and decide what topics we'd like to cover. Pizza will be served!

title: R-Ladies Meeting: R Markdown for reproducible reports
description: In this month's session, we will be viewing R Studio's webinar on R markdown. Bring your laptops to follow along. The materials for the meetup are found here: https://github.com/rstudio/webinars/tree/master/25-Notebooks. Please download them in advance!

title: R-Ladies Meeting: ggplot2 workshop
description:
What to expect: The focus of this month's meetup is ggplot2! Ggplot2 is a powerful R package for data visualization. The main benefit is the "grammar of graphics" that ggplot2 employs. In this workshop, we will learn ggplot2 via example. We'll provide the sample data to work with and create increasingly beautiful and complex visualizations. As a bonus, we'll also use the dplyr and plotly skills introduced last session.

What to bring: Please bring your laptops! Please see our GitHub page (https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal/tree/master/20180419_ggplot) for this workshop and follow the set up instructions *before* the meeting! We can definitely help out if you run into issues.

title: R-Ladies Kick-off: Meet, greet, treat + code!
description:
• What we'll do
At last, R-Ladies Montreal is a-go! We'll start with a round table for everyone to introduce themselves, followed by four 10 min. lightning sessions on R tips and tricks aimed at the new and intermediate R user. Then we'll open the floor and brainstorm topics for future sessions, have time for chatting, networking, and for folks to mention if they're hiring or looking for work. There will be pizza supplied by this meeting's sponsor, Precision Analytics -- thanks, ladies!

• What to bring
Bring a laptop if you want to install discuss packages and learn as you go. We aim to post the code of the lightning talks beforehand.

• Important to know
We have a code of conduct -- please read it: https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct

Happy coding :-)

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Holiday Break!
description:
Dear R-Ladies,

As many of us will, must likely, be on Holiday Break we have decided to postpone this meetup until the next one on January 16th.

Happy Holidays and Happy Coding :-)

title: R-Ladies Meeting: dplyr workshop
description:
*** PLEASE NOTE CHANGE OF LOCATION ***
1080 Cote du Beaver Hall, Montreal QC
Suite 200
*************************************************

We'll be holding a hands-on dplyr workshop. We'll present a brief intro to dplyr, and then have everyone work on a problem set of increasingly difficulty. It'll allow you to work alone and together using dplyr's many functions.

Please come with the most updated version of R and RStudio installed on your laptop. Please see our github page for more details on current and past events, including materials and code! https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal

As usual, snacks and drinks will be provided!!

Happy coding :-)

title: R-Ladies Meeting: DashR by Ryan Kyle
description:
Ryan Kyle will be giving a talk about Plotly's Dash for R package https://community.plot.ly/t/announcing-dash-for-r/25692. Dash for R creates beautiful web applications that allows you to harness the power of interactive analytics dashboards (learn more https://dash.plot.ly/).

Link to the material for the meetup https://github.com/rpkyle/rladies-mtl-demo

Link to the Dash for R github repo https://github.com/plotly/dashR!

Please come with the most updated version of R and RStudio installed on your laptop. Please see our github page for more details on current and past events, including materials and code! https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal

As usual, snacks and drinks will be provided!!

Happy coding :-)

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Introduction To R
description:
Hello RLadies,

With the best wishes and biggest consideration for everyone's vacation we are postponing this session until September.

Enjoy summer and Happy coding :-)

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Introduction To R
description:
Hello R-Ladies,

With the best wishes and biggest consideration for everyone's vacation we are postponing this session and the following August session until September.

Enjoy summer and Happy coding :-)

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Introduction To R
description:
We're so excited to host Isabella Gollini, PhD from University of Dublin https://sites.google.com/site/isabellagollini/

She'll be presenting an Introduction of R aimed at beginners!

Please come with the most updated version of R and RStudio installed on your laptop. Please see our github page for more details on current and past events, including materials and code! https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal

As usual, snacks and drinks will be provided!!

Happy coding :-)

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Topic to be confirmed.
description: Please come with the most updated version of R and RStudio installed on your laptop. Please see our github page for more details on current and past events, including materials and code! https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal

title: Introduction to Git and Github
description:
In preparation for this meetup, please arrive with the following:

- The most updated version of R - https://cran.r-project.org/
- RStudio - https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download/ installed on your laptop.
- Create a free Github account https://github.com/

Please see our github page for more details on current and past events, including materials and code! https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal

If you'd like to be added to our slack channel, please send me a message on the meetup platform. We share R tips, share details about conferences and events, and post jobs!

We'll have drinks and snacks 🍕

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Analyzing the impact of all-women tech groups
description:
Hi everyone!

At this month's R-Ladies meetup, I'll be presenting an analysis on the impact of women's tech groups (like this one!), on women's presence in the tech community. I'll be using some super cool publicly available data sets, making plots in ggplot2, and just having a great time exploring an interesting research question.

I'll have the R code, data and slides up by Thursday AM.

They'll be pizza, snacks and beers served :)
Can't wait to see you all there!

Erika

Please come with the most updated version of R and RStudio installed on your laptop. Please see our github page for more details on current and past events, including materials and code! https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Introduction to Machine Learning and Deep Learning
description: Please come with the most updated version of R and RStudio installed on your laptop. Please see our github page for more details on current and past events, including materials and code! https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Introduction to dplyr
description:
Come learn the ins- and outs of one of our favourite packages, dplyr, to learn loads about data manipulation
https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/

For meetup materials click here
https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal/tree/master/20190117_dplyr

Remember that R-Ladies Montreal is part of a worldwide organization to promote gender diversity in the R community. We seek to support women as they learn to use R, or expand their R toolkit, by hosting demos, workshops, and coding sessions. For more information, please send us a message through meetup.

All our slides can be found online: https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal

Food and drinks will be provided by our corporate sponsor Upgrade, Inc. https://www.upgrade.com/ and the space is provided by Plotly https://plot.ly/

title: R-Ladies Meeting: Has been Cancelled for the month of March.
description: After carefully reviewing the guidelines of the Government of Quebec and in the best interest of our guests and organizers we are proceeding with the cancellation of March’s R-Ladies Meet up. We will resume meet-ups as the ever-changing circumstances improve for the good of everyone.

title: Leaving academia and diving into data science! Myths, tips and tricks.
description:
In this talk, Erika Braithwaite will discuss some myths about jobs in data science and tricks about how to rebrand yourself if you're leaving academia. Erika will also present some analysis on the 2018 Kaggle Data Scientist Survey to help make talk more data-driven :)

Please come with the most updated version of R and RStudio installed on your laptop. Please see our github page for more details on current and past events, including materials and code! https://github.com/rladies/meetup-presentations_montreal

As usual, snacks and drinks will be provided by our sponsor Upgrade INC.!!

Happy coding :-)

title: Introduction to base R and ggplot2
description:
***\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*IMPORTANT\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\****

**DATE:** TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 28th

**LOCATION:** ROOM 189, 550 RUE SHERBROOKE OUEST (H3A 1B9), IN DOWNTOWN MONTRÉAL

***\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*IMPORTANT\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\****

## Details

Join us on **TUESDAY November 28th** for our final meetup of the year!

**Agenda:**

* Settling in and and request for talks
* Speaker
* Networking / practical coding time

**Topic:**
This month we'll be discussing commonly used base R commands and an introduction to the R package ggplot2 for visualizing data.

This session, we will work through exercises related to:

* essential commands in base R (getting started, handling data, etc.)
* visualizing data in graphs using ggplot2
* exploring resources outside of Rstudio/Posit to support your R learning

While this is a more introductory R meetup, all levels are welcome!
Experienced R-users are encouraged to help new R users!

**What to bring:**
Bring your laptop if you'd like to follow along with the examples. We recommend installing the latest version of [R](https://www.r-project.org/) and [RStudio](https://posit.co/products/open-source/rstudio/) before you arrive.

**Other:**
R-Ladies events always have been and will always be free to attend! RSVPs are not required, but do help us plan space requirements. Please note have changed our location to McGill campus at 550 rue Sherbrooke for this meetup, with space generously provided by the Quantitative Life Sciences department. See above for location details.

**Questions?**
Please comment or message the organizers with any questions.

title: RLadies October Meetup - "R-omics": Using R for bioinformatics
description:
Join us on October 30th for our second meetup of the 2023-24 season!

**Agenda:**

* Settling in and and request for talks
* Speaker
* Networking / practical coding time

**Topic:**
This month we'll be discussing how to use R for bioinformatics. Bioinformatics is a rapidly evolving field that combines aspects of data science, biostatistics, and natural sciences in order to analyze biological datasets. Many research areas can fall under the umbrella of bioinformatics, from modelling the 3D structure of proteins to identifying gene mutations associated with disease.

This session, we will work through exercises related to:

* essential resources for working with genomic data, like Bioconductor packages
* visualizing high-dimensional data such as spatial transcriptomics
* calling command-line tools from R

While some biology background will be helpful for this session, everyone is welcome.

**What to bring:**
Bring your laptop if you'd like to follow along with the examples. We recommend installing the latest version of [R](https://www.r-project.org/) and [RStudio](https://posit.co/products/open-source/rstudio/) before you arrive.

**Other:**
R-Ladies events always have been and will always be free to attend! RSVPs are not required, but do help us plan space requirements.

**Questions?**
Please comment or message the organizers with any questions.

title: RLadies 2023-24 Kickoff: Introduction to data wrangling with dplyr
description:
Join us on September 25 as we kick-off R-Ladies for the 2023-24 season!

**Agenda:**

* Welcome and introductions

* Some history about R-Ladies Montreal and request for talks

* Introduction to data wrangling using dplyr

* Networking / practical coding time

For this introduction to data wrangling in dplyr, we assume only a basic level of R knowledge, but all are welcome. If you are proficient in R, we encourage you to attend for the planning, networking, and ideally to help out your fellow useRs! Ideally come with R and RStudio already installed, as well as the dplyr package, but we can help troubleshoot as needed.

The event will be held at WeWork L'avenue, on the 5th floor just past reception.

Please comment or message the organizers with any questions.

R-Ladies events always have been and will always be free to attend! RSVPs are not required, but do help us plan space requirements.

title: RLadies February Meetup - Introduction to R and RStudio
description:
Join us on Tuesday, February 27th for an intro to R night!
Please note our location for this meetup has changed to:

Room 189
550 Rue Sherbrooke O
Montréal, QC H3A 1B9

**Agenda:**

* Settling in and and request for talks
* Speaker
* Networking / practical coding time

**Topic:**
In this session, we will first discuss how to get started in R language for new users and paths to careers in coding. We will then learn about the basics of R language and how to use RStudio integrated development environment (IDE). We will learn how to import and do basic manipulations of data sets, and how to then visualize these datasets in graphs using ggplot2 package. Feel free to bring your own datasets to test out! Toy datasets will also be provided through our Github page.

There will be pizza!!

**What to bring:**
Bring your laptop if you'd like to follow along with the examples. We recommend installing the latest version of [R](https://www.r-project.org/) and [RStudio](https://posit.co/products/open-source/rstudio/) before you arrive.

**Other:**
R-Ladies events always have been and will always be free to attend! RSVPs are not required, but do help us plan space requirements. We would like to thank the Quantitative Life Sciences department at McGill University for hosting this session!

**Questions?**
Please comment or message the organizers with any questions.

title: RLadies January Meetup - Introduction to Python in RStudio
description:
Join us on Tuesday, January 30th for our first meetup of 2024!
Please note our location for this meetup has changed to:

Room 189
550 Rue Sherbrooke O
Montréal, QC H3A 1B9

**Agenda:**

* Settling in and and request for talks
* Speaker
* Networking / practical coding time

**Topic:**
In this session, we will first go over the basics of the Python Programming Language. Next, we will learn to use Python modules for efficient data wrangling and visualization. And finally, we will cover the different methods to implement Python code into RStudio.

**What to bring:**
Bring your laptop if you'd like to follow along with the examples. We recommend installing the latest version of [R](https://www.r-project.org/) and [RStudio](https://posit.co/products/open-source/rstudio/) before you arrive. Installation of [Python](https://www.python.org/downloads/) is highly recommended for practicing Python code during this session's tutorial.

**Other:**
R-Ladies events always have been and will always be free to attend! RSVPs are not required, but do help us plan space requirements. We would like to thank the Quantitative Life Sciences department at McGill University for hosting this session!

**Questions?**
Please comment or message the organizers with any questions.

title: R-Ladies Montreal: Reboot + Refresh Virtual Meetup
description:
Monday, June 16 @ 7:00pm

Let’s shape the future of our local R & data science community together!
We're bringing R-Ladies Montreal back to life—and we want your input!

Join us for a casual, community-focused **virtual** meetup on June 16, where we will:
\- Share plans for rebooting the group and gather your feedback
\- Discuss the results from our recent community survey
\- Brainstorm ideas for future events \(in\-person and virtual\)
\- Enjoy two short\, fun presentations on interesting R packages
We will also preview and plan upcoming events.
Whether you're brand new to R or a longtime data nerd, your voice matters. Help us build the kind of community you want to be part of!
